title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Shortness Of Breath
intro: Herbal teas for shortness of breath are a type of tea that is consumed to alleviate symptoms of respiratory distress, characterized by difficulty breathing. Herbal teas can treat shortness of breath due to their ability to relax the airways, reduce inflammation, and improve lung function. Examples of herbal teas that can help alleviate shortness of breath include Peppermint tea, which eases congestion and opens airways; Thyme tea, which has antispasmodic properties to relax the muscles in the airways; Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and improves circulation; Licorice root tea, which soothes the respiratory tract and reduces coughing; and Eucalyptus tea, which eases congestion and opens airways. Additionally, herbal teas such as Echinacea tea, Rosemary tea, and Sage tea also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the airways and improve breathing.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath, including those associated with respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and protect lung tissue from oxidative stress, making it easier to breathe. Bioactive constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides in Echinacea purpurea teas are responsible for these beneficial effects, promoting a decrease in airway resistance and congestion. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals may experience relief from shortness of breath due to the herbal preparation's ability to calm the respiratory system and promote overall lung health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea.
Add the 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Echinacea purpurea teas may have potential for adjuvant symptomatic therapy in respiratory conditions, including shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat shortness of breath, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, as well as stomach upset and nausea due to the high content of volatile compounds. To safely use Echinacea purpurea teas for this purpose, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large quantities, as excessive intake can exacerbate breathing difficulties, and also to avoid mixing it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-10 minutes and then strain the tea
Consume for 7-10 days to experience potential benefits
Consult a doctor before using for extended periods or high dosages

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, made from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation in the airways and protect against oxidative stress, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, act as bronchodilators, relaxing the airway muscles and improving airflow. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help to reduce symptoms of shortness of breath, improve respiratory function, and enhance overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and place them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea slowly, ideally 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed for shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland)", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for shortness of breath may potentially be beneficial due to the antifibrotic properties of Rosmarinus officinalis, which has been shown to attenuate fibrosis in various organs, including the lungs, in experimental animal models.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating shortness of breath, but consuming them may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas effectively, it's essential to start with small doses, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid using it before bedtime to minimize potential sleep disruptions.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis tea for shortness of breath.
Drink 1-2 cups of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with small dose (1/2 cup) and gradually increase as needed.
Take tea 30 minutes before meals or as directed by doctor.
Monitor breathing and adjust tea usage or consult doctor if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used traditionally to treat shortness of breath due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to improve circulation and relax blood vessels, allowing for easier breathing. This herbal preparation can be particularly beneficial in treating shortness of breath caused by conditions such as chronic bronchitis and asthma, as it helps to reduce inflammation and open airways. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas may also help to improve overall respiratory function and reduce symptoms of shortness of breath.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and place it in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to alleviate shortness of breath, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions, particularly if consumed in large quantities or by individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and be cautious when consuming Ginkgo biloba teas with other medications or substances that may interact with it.
preparation_usage: Start by drinking 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink the tea before meals or as needed for shortness of breath.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage to 1-2 cups per day if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat the shortness of breath ailment, also known as respiratory distress, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This herbal preparation helps to alleviate respiratory issues by reducing inflammation in the airways, promoting the clearance of mucus, and relaxing bronchial muscles. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and enhancing the body's natural antioxidant defenses. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat shortness of breath include improved lung function, reduced coughing and wheezing, and enhanced overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and a cup of boiling water.
Add the Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the root. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate shortness of breath.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for an extended period or if you have any underlying health conditions.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BioMed research international", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for shortness of breath show significant potential therapeutic activity as one of the plants with higher use value, indicating its potential effectiveness in treating respiratory ailments.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be effective in treating shortness of breath, but it may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and headaches due to its licorice root content. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, take them in moderation, monitor your blood pressure and electrolyte levels, and avoid using them for extended periods to minimize the risk of adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes and strain before drinking.
Continue for 7-10 days for optimal results.
Consult a doctor for proper dosage and usage guidelines.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, ginger teas can alleviate shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory function. Regular consumption of ginger teas may also help to prevent exacerbations of respiratory conditions, such as asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), thereby providing long-term benefits in managing this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and add it to the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Zingiber officinale root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Zingiber officinale root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from shortness of breath, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas, particularly those with high antioxidant activity, such as those with 222.30mg/TE/g DW DPPH, 98.04mg/TE/g DW FRAP, 38.58mg/NGN/g DW flavonoid, 10.78mg/GAE/g DW phenolic acid and 22.84mg/GAE/g DW polyphenol contents, may be beneficial in alleviating shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat the shortness of breath ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its spicy properties and potential interaction with other medications. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, individuals should take it in moderation, start with small doses, and avoid consuming it before bedtime or on an empty stomach to minimize potential side effects like stomach discomfort and indigestion.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for shortness of breath
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger per cup of boiling water
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed
Continue usage for 7-10 days to observe improvement

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to its calming and relaxing properties. The tea's ability to reduce stress and anxiety helps to alleviate respiratory issues, allowing the body to breathe more easily. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, found in Lavandula angustifolia teas, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to soothe the airways and improve lung function. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals may experience a reduction in shortness of breath and improved overall respiratory health, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for those suffering from this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can help alleviate shortness of breath symptoms when consumed, but its use may lead to potential side effects such as drowsiness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, one should start with small doses, gradually increase the amount, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications, particularly those that slow down breathing or interact with herbal supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Reduce frequency if shortness of breath improves within 2-3 days.
Maintain 1-2 cups per day to manage shortness of breath.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after 7 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath, particularly in cases of bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparation's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as eucalyptol, help to ease respiratory congestion and reduce airway inflammation. By thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the lungs, eucalyptus globulus teas assist in improving airflow and making breathing easier, providing relief from shortness of breath. The benefits of using eucalyptus globulus teas to treat this ailment include reduced respiratory symptoms, improved lung function, and a decrease in the severity of asthma attacks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan or kettle.
Add the eucalyptus leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the liquid.
Drink the tea immediately, 2-3 times a day for relief from shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas may be beneficial for shortness of breath as it was one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory disorders in Ethiopia, particularly for cough, which often accompanies shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to help alleviate shortness of breath by relieving congestion in the airways, but may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To safely use Eucalyptus globulus teas for this purpose, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid taking it before bedtime, and be cautious when consuming it if you have a history of allergies or are pregnant or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dosage of 5-7 grams of dried leaves per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 10-15 grams if needed.
Steep tea for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Use tea for 3-6 months to manage shortness of breath symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath, a symptom often associated with respiratory and cardiovascular conditions, due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. The adaptogenic properties of Panax ginseng teas help to improve overall cardiovascular function, increase oxygenation of the body, and enhance physical performance, thus alleviating shortness of breath. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, including ginsenosides and panaxans, have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, improve blood circulation, and reduce inflammation, all of which contribute to the treatment of shortness of breath. By consuming Panax ginseng teas, individuals can benefit from improved respiratory and cardiovascular function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a potential natural remedy for shortness of breath.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot.
Reduce heat to low and add the dried ginseng root. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginseng root. Add 1 tablespoon of honey if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The European respiratory journal", Panax ginseng teas may be effective in alleviating shortness of breath in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), as significant intergroup differences for one or more outcomes were reported in trials testing its efficacy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath; however, possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and insomnia due to its stimulating properties. To use Panax ginseng teas safely, individuals should be cautious of interactions with medications, such as blood thinners, and start with low doses to gradually adjust to its effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Panax ginseng tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon ginseng powder per 8 oz water.
Gradually increase to 1-2 tablespoons per cup as needed.
Use ginseng tea for 2-4 weeks to see improvement in shortness of breath.
Consult a doctor if shortness of breath persists or worsens.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to its expectorant properties that help to ease congestion and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents, such as carvacrol and thymol, in thyme tea are responsible for its medicinal effects, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can exacerbate respiratory issues. Thymus vulgaris teas help to treat shortness of breath by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from respiratory distress. The benefits of thyme tea in treating shortness of breath include improved lung function, reduced coughing and congestion, and a general sense of well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the leaves from the water using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, when experiencing shortness of breath.
Consult a doctor before using Thymus vulgaris tea for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Thymus vulgaris teas may be effective in alleviating shortness of breath due to the spasmolytic and antimicrobial properties of thymol contained in the volatile thyme oil, which can help in relieving respiratory symptoms associated with upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to help alleviate shortness of breath, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in rare cases. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat shortness of breath, take precautions such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, and being cautious when consuming other medications or substances that may interact with the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Thymus vulgaris tea for shortness of breath.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 2-3 cups of Thymus vulgaris tea per day, 2 times a day.
Continue for 7-10 days, then reassess your condition with your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath, a symptom often associated with respiratory diseases. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, thereby alleviating the symptoms of shortness of breath. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, in Astragalus membranaceus teas have been shown to have immunomodulatory and adaptogenic effects, which help to boost the immune system and increase lung function. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus teas has been reported to improve respiratory function, increase oxygenation, and reduce fatigue, providing relief from shortness of breath and improving overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Astragalus membranaceus root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for shortness of breath relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic", Astragalus membranaceus teas may have therapeutic benefit for shortness of breath associated with angina, as indicated by human trials where a combination of Inula racemosa and Commiphora mukul, along with Astragalus membranaceus, was shown to be superior to nitroglycerin in reducing chest pain and dyspnea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can help alleviate shortness of breath by improving lung function and reducing inflammation, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Astragalus membranaceus teas safely, take it in moderation, avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal supplements, and be cautious when using it with underlying conditions such as high blood pressure or kidney disease.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a small dose of 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup.
Gradually increase the dose to 2-3 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Continue usage for 2-4 weeks to observe improvement in shortness of breath.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage and frequency as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to treat the ailment by loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs, thereby reducing congestion and improving breathing. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra teas, including flavonoids, terpenes, and glycosides, contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the relaxation of airway smooth muscles. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat shortness of breath include improved respiratory function, reduced coughing, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Sambucus nigra dried flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried flowers in a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the flowers.
Drink 1 cup of the Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours and reheat as needed.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to potentially alleviate shortness of breath due to its expectorant properties, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat shortness of breath, such as avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring blood pressure, and being cautious when combining it with other medications that lower blood pressure.

Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Adjust dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup if symptoms persist.
Use Sambucus nigra tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable relief.
Combine with other natural remedies for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as anise or fennel tea, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The bioactive constituents of fennel tea, including volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone, act as bronchodilators, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, fennel tea helps to alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory function. The benefits of fennel tea in treating shortness of breath include improved lung function, reduced coughing, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare (anise) leaves and flowers. You can use dried anise instead.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the fresh or dried anise and place it in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot until it boils. Let it cool for 1 minute.
Pour the boiling water over the anise in the cup or teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the anise. Drink the tea while it's still warm to help relieve shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of integrative medicine, Foeniculum vulgare teas for shortness of breath were not mentioned in the provided study, as it only discussed its potential use in treating acute cough.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat shortness of breath, but its possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Foeniculum vulgare teas, including drinking it in moderation and avoiding it if you have any underlying medical conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, or if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.

The properties of Schisandra chinensis teas that help to treat this ailment include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis teas, such as schisandrin and gomisin A, help to reduce inflammation, improve lung function, and increase oxygenation, thereby alleviating shortness of breath. Regular consumption of Schisandra chinensis teas has been shown to provide significant benefits in treating shortness of breath, including improved lung function, increased exercise tolerance, and enhanced overall quality of life.

Add the 1 tablespoon of dried berries to the boiling water.

To use Schisandra chinensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach or with other stimulants.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon teas, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as cinnamaldehyde and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax the airway muscles and improve breathing. By reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus, Cinnamomum verum teas help to alleviate shortness of breath and improve respiratory function. Regular consumption of Ceylon cinnamon teas has been found to provide benefits in treating shortness of breath, including improved lung function, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon cinnamon) bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup or mug.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey or your preferred sweetener (optional).
Add the Ceylon cinnamon to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into the cup or mug and discard the cinnamon bark.
Add the measured honey or sweetener to the tea and stir well.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be beneficial in treating shortness of breath due to its expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Cinnamomum verum teas include consuming it in moderation, avoiding it in people with sensitive stomachs, and not using it as a replacement for conventional medical treatment without proper understanding of its effects on the body.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by relaxing the airways, improving lung function, and reducing inflammation, thereby making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, such as theaflavins, thearubigins, and catechins, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which include reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune response. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat shortness of breath include improved respiratory function, reduced symptoms of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Measure 1 teaspoon of tea leaves and add to the tea infuser.
Steep the tea for 3-5 minutes in boiling water.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help alleviate shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents, Camellia sinensis teas have shown significant antioxidant capacity, particularly in its 50% methanolic extract, which was found to be the most potent with a radical scavenging DPPH assay of 78.95±7.12%, indicating its potential protective effects against oxidative injury associated with shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath, but potential side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and an increased heart rate due to the caffeine content. To use Camellia sinensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid consuming them in the evening, and be mindful of any pre-existing heart conditions, as the caffeine can exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, made from the turmeric root, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath by alleviating inflammation and improving respiratory function. The bioactive constituents, including curcuminoids and polyphenols, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs and airways. By inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and promoting the regeneration of damaged lung tissue, Curcuma longa teas can help to improve lung function and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory health, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for treating shortness of breath.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tsp of dried Curcuma longa root, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add the 1 tsp of dried Curcuma longa root to the tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Curcuma longa tea 2-3 times a day for shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can help alleviate shortness of breath by reducing inflammation and improving lung function, but its use may be associated with side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high polyphenol content. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor liver enzymes, and avoid taking Curcuma longa teas with other medications that may interact with its bioactive compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath, a symptom often associated with respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting the removal of mucus, thereby easing breathing. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune response and improving lung function. The benefits of using Paeonia lactiflora teas to treat shortness of breath include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a decrease in the severity and frequency of respiratory episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Paeonia lactiflora flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for shortness of breath relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used to treat various conditions, but there is no specific information provided about their use for shortness of breath, and more research is needed to understand their potential benefits and mechanisms of action in this context.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used to treat shortness of breath due to its potential to dilate blood vessels and reduce inflammation, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions. Precautions should be taken when using Paeonia lactiflora teas, such as avoiding its use in individuals with bleeding disorders or those taking anticoagulant medications, as well as not consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods to prevent adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and improving breathing. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum teas, including thymol and carvacrol, possess antimicrobial and antispasmodic activities that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the airways. By consuming Thymus serpyllum teas, individuals can benefit from improved respiratory function, reduced symptoms of shortness of breath, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers. You can find them in health food stores or grow them yourself.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers (or 2 tablespoons of fresh ones). You can dry them by air-drying or using a food dehydrator.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried Thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers in a teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve shortness of breath. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but not too much sugar or acidity.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat shortness of breath, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include starting with small doses, being cautious when mixing it with other medications, and avoiding its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to alleviate shortness of breath, also known as dyspnea, in individuals suffering from various respiratory conditions. The sedative properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate shortness of breath. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, possess anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax the airway muscles and improve lung function. By consuming Passiflora incarnata teas, individuals can experience relief from shortness of breath, improved sleep quality, and reduced symptoms of anxiety and stress.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Passiflora incarnata flowers or 1 cup of dried flowers.
Steep 1 cup of boiling water over 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat shortness of breath, but may cause side effects such as dizziness, drowsiness, and nausea due to its sedative properties. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea too close to bedtime or when operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas have been traditionally used to treat shortness of breath due to their bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax and expand the airways, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra sinica teas, including ephedrine, ephedradine, and berberine, play a crucial role in treating this ailment by improving respiratory function and reducing inflammation. By increasing oxygen flow and reducing congestion, Ephedra sinica teas help to alleviate shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory health. The benefits of using Ephedra sinica teas to treat shortness of breath include improved lung function, reduced symptoms of respiratory distress, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and flowers.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day for shortness of breath.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Ephedra sinica teas for shortness of breath may not have been directly mentioned in this study, however, Ephedra was listed as one of the herbs associated with significant adverse effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can be used to treat shortness of breath, but possible side effects may include increased heart rate, high blood pressure, and insomnia, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential risks, it's essential to use these teas in moderation, ideally under the guidance of a traditional practitioner, and be aware of any adverse reactions, such as headaches or tremors.
